name: Avadian
description_short: Avadian Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union operating 18 locations across Alabama with online banking, personal and business lending, and investment services.
description_long:
Avadian Credit Union is one of Alabama's oldest credit unions, celebrating its 92nd annual meeting in 2024, operating as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution. The credit union maintains 18 physical locations throughout Alabama and serves both personal and business members through a comprehensive digital banking platform. Founded on a commitment to prioritizing member interests over profits, Avadian has built longevity through this member-first philosophy and maintains federal NCUA insurance protection supplemented by Excess Share Insurance Corporation coverage up to $250,000 on savings accounts. The organization operates under routing number 262087528 and NMLS #422274.

Avadian offers a broad range of financial products across deposit, lending, and investment categories. On the deposit side, members access personal and business checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs). Lending services include auto loans, boat loans, motorcycle loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), home improvement loans, personal loans, and business loans including commercial real estate and business term loans. The credit union issues the Avadian Visa Signature Card earning 1.5 reward points per dollar spent with a 20,000 bonus points promotion. Business services extend to commercial analysis checking, merchant services, treasury management tools, payroll services, and remote deposit capture.

Avadian distinguishes itself through its long institutional history in Alabama, member-focused governance with a volunteer board, community outreach and development programs, and the Avadian Foundation. The organization emphasizes fraud prevention education and security, explicitly stating that Avadian will never email, call, or text members requesting online banking credentials, account numbers, or Social Security Numbers. They provide accessible online and telephone banking, Zelle payment capability, and Autobooks integration for business invoice and payment management. The credit union offers employee group benefits and maintains a dedicated careers section highlighting company culture and hiring processes.

Avadian operates as a legitimate credit union with federal oversight and deposit insurance, making it suitable for Alabama residents seeking member-owned banking alternatives. The $10,000 minimum opening deposit requirement (in funds not held at Avadian in the past 30 days) positions it toward customers with substantial deposits rather than those seeking minimal-barrier accounts. While the website demonstrates comprehensive service offerings and security awareness, specific details about APR ranges, fee structures, and loan approval requirements are not prominently displayed on the homepage, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union or review full fee schedules for detailed comparison.
services: Personal checking and savings accounts with online and telephone banking access
Money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Auto loans, boat loans, and motorcycle loans
Mortgages and home equity lines of credit (HELOCs)
Home improvement loans and personal loans
Avadian Visa Signature Credit Card with 1.5 reward points per dollar spent
Business checking (including Commercial Analysis Checking), business savings, and business investments
Commercial real estate loans, business term loans, and business lines of credit
Business credit cards and merchant services
Treasury management tools and payroll services
Remote deposit capture for business accounts
Zelle payment capability and bill payment services
pros: NCUA federal insurance with additional Excess Share Insurance Corporation coverage up to $250,000 on savings accounts
92-year institutional history as Alabama's oldest credit union with established member trust and community presence
Comprehensive service suite including personal/business checking, savings, mortgages, auto loans, business loans, and treasury management
Member-first governance model with volunteer board and commitment to prioritizing member interests over profits
Avadian Visa Signature Card earns 1.5 reward points per dollar with 20,000 bonus points promotion
18 physical Alabama locations plus online banking platform with 24/7 access via Zelle payments and telephone banking
Strong fraud prevention education and transparent security policies (no unsolicited requests for credentials)
cons: High $10,000 minimum opening deposit requirement excludes customers seeking low-barrier entry accounts
Limited geographic availability restricted to Alabama with 18 branch locations (no nationwide presence)
Homepage does not display specific APR ranges, loan terms, or detailed fee schedules, requiring direct contact for rate information
Geographically limited membership eligibility as credit union membership is typically restricted to residents/employees in service area
Limited mobile app functionality details visible on website; primary emphasis on online/telephone banking rather than mobile-first platform
best_for: Alabama residents with substantial deposits ($10,000+) seeking member-owned banking with federal insurance and local branch access
Small business owners in Alabama needing commercial checking, business loans, payroll services, and treasury management tools
Home buyers and mortgage refinance seekers prioritizing credit union lending rates and member-focused customer service
Members valuing long-term institutional stability, community involvement, and member-governance banking models
diagnosis: Avadian Credit Union is best suited for Alabama residents and small business owners with substantial deposits ($10,000+) seeking federally-insured, member-owned banking with 92 years of institutional credibility and comprehensive lending options. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Alabama with 18 branch locations and a higher minimum opening deposit than most competitors, which restricts accessibility for new customers with smaller initial deposits or those outside the service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Avadian — NCUA-insured credit union in Birmingham, AL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
